proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months across the full cohort.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months across the full cohort.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months across the full cohort.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months across the full cohort.
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months across the full cohort.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months across the full cohort.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months across the full cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Age has a strong POSITIVE Pearson correlation with pfs_months (r=0.757, p<1e-300). Quartile means rise monotonically: Q1 (30-58y) 1.33mo, Q2 (58-65y) 2.78mo, Q3 (65-72y) 3.97mo, Q4 (72-90y) 5.69mo.
Albumin is positively correlated with pfs_months (r=0.099, p=2.8e-109).
Weight loss is negatively correlated with pfs_months (r=-0.127, p=7.5e-179).
LDH has a small negative correlation with pfs_months (r=-0.019, p=3.5e-5).
PD-L1 TPS shows no significant overall correlation with pfs_months (r=0.006, p=0.157).
NLR shows no overall correlation with pfs_months (r=0.004, p=0.42).
CRP shows no overall correlation with pfs_months (r=0.001, p=0.78).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with has_brain_mets=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Squamous histology is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter pfs_months than males.
Patients with tmb_high=1 have longer pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: stage_iv=1: mean PFS 2.93 vs stage_iv=0: 4.36, diff=-1.44 mo, p<1e-300.
has_brain_mets=1: 2.74 vs 0: 3.66, diff=-0.92 mo, p<1e-300.
ECOG slope coef=-1.099 mo per ECOG unit, p<1e-300.
Squamous: 2.81 vs adenocarcinoma: 3.67, diff=-0.86 mo (highly significant).
current 2.99, former 3.61, never 3.68. Current smokers ~0.6-0.7 mo lower (highly significant).
Female 3.32 vs male 3.52, diff=-0.20 mo, p=7.7e-25. Hypothesis supported but small.
tmb_high=1: 3.30 vs tmb_high=0: 3.49, diff=-0.19 mo, p=1.0e-17. Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis: TMB-high is mildly negatively prognostic in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 increases mean pfs_months versus treatment_pembrolizumab=0 in the overall cohort.
treatment_sotorasib=1 increases mean pfs_months versus treatment_sotorasib=0 in the overall cohort.
treatment_olaparib=1 increases mean pfs_months versus treatment_olaparib=0 in the overall cohort.
treatment_osimertinib=1 increases mean pfs_months versus treatment_osimertinib=0 in the overall cohort.
Treatment assignment appears randomized: biomarker prevalence does not differ between treated and untreated groups for any of the four treatments.

analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab: on 3.41 vs off 3.45, diff=-0.035 mo, p=0.081. NULL/possibly slightly negative main effect.
Sotorasib: on 3.64 vs off 3.32, diff=+0.322 mo, p=5.6e-49. Main effect positive (likely driven by biomarker subgroup).
Olaparib: on 3.41 vs off 3.44, diff=-0.025 mo, p=0.26. NULL main effect.
Osimertinib: on 3.43 vs off 3.43, diff=+0.004, p=0.87. NULL main effect.

analyses.code: Compare biomarker prevalence on/off each treatment
analyses.result_summary: egfr, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11, brca2, tmb_high prevalences differ by <0.5 percentage points on/off any treatment. Treatment assignment appears randomized — supports causal interpretation of subgroup effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_osimertinib increases pfs_months specifically in patients with egfr_mutation=1 (positive interaction term in pfs_months ~ treatment_osimertinib * egfr_mutation).
treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months specifically in patients with kras_g12c=1 (positive interaction term).
treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months specifically in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (positive interaction term).
treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months more in patients with tmb_high=1 (positive interaction term).
treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months more in patients with high pdl1_tps (positive interaction term).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef=-0.020, p=0.76. NO significant treatment_osimertinib×egfr_mutation interaction. Hypothesis refuted in this dataset.

Interaction coef=+2.558, p<1e-300. STRONG POSITIVE sotorasib×kras_g12c interaction. KRAS+/sotorasib mean PFS 5.77 vs KRAS+/no-sotorasib 3.21 (diff +2.56 mo); KRAS-/sotorasib 3.33 vs KRAS-/no-sotorasib 3.33 (no effect).
Interaction coef=+0.037, p=0.78. NO significant olaparib×brca2 interaction. BRCA2+/olaparib 3.32 vs BRCA2+/no-olaparib 3.31 (essentially identical).
Interaction coef=-0.018, p=0.69. NO pembrolizumab×TMB interaction.
Interaction coef=-0.116, p=0.22 (direction OPPOSITE — slightly less benefit with high PDL1, but not significant).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, treatment_sotorasib increases mean pfs_months by ~2.5 months relative to no sotorasib.
Within the egfr_mutation=1 subgroup, treatment_osimertinib does not change mean pfs_months.
Within the brca2_mutation=1 subgroup, treatment_olaparib does not change mean pfs_months.
Within the tmb_high=1 subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab does not change mean pfs_months.
Within the alk_fusion=1 subgroup, treatment_osimertinib increases mean pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest in subset df[kras_g12c==1] by treatment_sotorasib
analyses.result_summary: KRAS+ sotorasib n=2246 mean 5.77; off n=4122 mean 3.21; diff=+2.55 mo, p~0. Hypothesis strongly supported.

analyses.code: ttest in subset df[egfr_mutation==1] by treatment_osimertinib
analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ osimertinib n=1973 mean 3.59; off n=4544 mean 3.60; diff=-0.014 mo, p=0.81. Null effect confirmed — surprising for this canonical pairing.

analyses.code: ttest in subset df[brca2_mutation==1] by treatment_olaparib
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ olaparib n=417 mean 3.32; off n=1102 mean 3.31; diff=+0.010 mo, p=0.93. Null effect confirmed.

analyses.code: ttest in subset df[tmb_high==1] by treatment_pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: TMB-high pembro n=7111 mean 3.27; off n=7147 mean 3.32; diff=-0.048 mo, p=0.20. Null (slightly negative) effect.

analyses.code: ttest in subset df[alk_fusion==1] by treatment_osimertinib
analyses.result_summary: ALK+ osimertinib n=751 mean 2.82; off n=1713 mean 2.59; diff=+0.231 mo, p=0.018. Marginal positive effect; small magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, the sotorasib treatment effect is modified by stk11_mutation: sotorasib benefit is smaller in stk11_mutation=1 patients than in stk11_mutation=0 (negative 3-way interaction).
Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, the sotorasib treatment effect is larger in patients without brain metastases (has_brain_mets=0) than with brain metastases.
Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, the sotorasib treatment effect differs by histology (adenocarcinoma vs squamous).

analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction coef=-0.271, p=0.13 (NS). Within KRAS+: stk11=0 sotorasib effect +2.59, stk11=1 +2.33. STK11 does NOT meaningfully suppress sotorasib benefit in this dataset.

analyses.code: Stratified means in KRAS+: brain mets 0 vs 1
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS+: brain_mets=0 sotorasib effect +2.63, brain_mets=1 +2.33. Slight attenuation but not striking.

analyses.code: Stratified means in KRAS+ by histology
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS+: adenocarcinoma sotorasib effect +2.62, squamous +2.50. Effect roughly equivalent across histologies.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, the sotorasib treatment effect on pfs_months differs sharply between males and females, with substantially larger benefit in males.

analyses.code: Stratified means in KRAS+ by sex_female
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS+: sex_female=0 (males) sotorasib n=1237 mean 7.88, off 3.23, diff=+4.64 mo, p<1e-300. sex_female=1 (females) sotorasib n=1009 mean 3.18, off 3.19, diff=-0.01 mo, p=0.88. STRIKING sex modification: sotorasib benefit is concentrated entirely in males.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The sotorasib effect modification by sex within KRAS+ is captured by a significant 3-way interaction in pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib * kras_g12c * sex_female: positive 2-way (sotorasib:kras_g12c) and negative 3-way (sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female).

analyses.result_summary: treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c coef=+4.663 (p<1e-300); treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female coef=-4.688 (p<1e-300). The 3-way interaction essentially cancels the 2-way: sotorasib benefit in KRAS+ patients is virtually zero in females.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS+ males, the sotorasib treatment effect on pfs_months increases with age_years (positive sotorasib×age interaction).
The 4-way interaction treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female × age_years is significant (sex_female attenuates the kras+ × age sotorasib effect).

analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS+ males: sotorasib coef=+2.57 mo at age 0 baseline; sotorasib×age coef=+0.030 mo per year (p=1.9e-9). At age 65, effect ≈ 4.5 mo; at age 75, ≈ 4.8 mo; quartile-stratified diffs: <58.5y +4.07, 58.5-65 +4.67, 65-72 +4.52, >72y +4.92.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib * kras_g12c * sex_female * age_years', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:age_years coef=+0.0315 (p=5.7e-11); treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female:age_years coef=-0.0375 (p=1.5e-7). Confirms the age modification of the sotorasib×kras effect is itself blunted by female sex.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists at least one feature that significantly modifies the pembrolizumab treatment effect on pfs_months (interaction p<0.05) when screened across all 20 candidate features.

analyses.code: Loop: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * X', data=df) for each candidate feature X
analyses.result_summary: Of 20 candidate moderators, only two show interaction p<0.05: stage_iv (coef=+0.108, p=0.007) — pembrolizumab is slightly less harmful in stage IV; weight_loss_pct_6mo (coef=+0.018 per 1% weight loss, p=3e-4) — pembrolizumab's slightly negative effect attenuates as weight loss rises. No biomarker or PDL1/TMB interaction found. Magnitudes are small (<0.15 mo at typical values).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab provides a positive treatment effect on pfs_months in at least one single-stratum subgroup (sex, smoking, ECOG, histology, stage_iv, brain mets, EGFR, KRAS, ALK, STK11, BRCA2, or TMB-high).

analyses.code: Stratified ttest for treatment_pembrolizumab across each single-stratum subgroup
analyses.result_summary: In every single-stratum subgroup checked the pembrolizumab on-vs-off mean PFS difference is between -0.13 and +0.05 months. The hypothesis of a positive treatment effect in any subgroup is REFUTED. Pembrolizumab shows no benefit in any clinical or biomarker stratum in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists at least one feature in the dataset (any candidate moderator) that significantly modifies the olaparib treatment effect on pfs_months at p<0.01.
Olaparib provides a positive treatment effect on pfs_months in at least one single-stratum subgroup.

analyses.code: Loop: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib * X', data=df) for each candidate X
analyses.result_summary: Across all 20 candidate moderators, no interaction term reached p<0.01. Olaparib effect is essentially flat across patient features.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest for treatment_olaparib across each single-stratum subgroup
analyses.result_summary: No subgroup shows a positive olaparib effect with |diff|>0.3 mo. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists a subgroup (defined by any feature combination) in which osimertinib provides a positive treatment effect on pfs_months larger than 0.3 months.
The osimertinib×egfr_mutation effect, if absent overall, may emerge in a sub-stratum defined by an additional feature (3-way interaction screen).

analyses.code: Stratified ttest for treatment_osimertinib across single-stratum subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Only signals: alk_fusion=1 +0.231 mo (p=0.018, marginal); brca2_mutation=1 -0.243 (p=0.039). No other subgroup shows |diff|>0.3 mo. The marginal ALK+ signal is the closest thing to a positive osimertinib subgroup.

analyses.code: Loop: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_osimertinib * egfr_mutation * X', data=df) for each candidate X
analyses.result_summary: No significant 3-way interactions detected (all p>0.05). The osimertinib×EGFR pairing is genuinely null in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable model adjusted for age, sex, ECOG, histology, stage IV, brain mets, lab markers, smoking, and biomarkers, only treatment_sotorasib retains a significant positive coefficient on pfs_months among the four treatments.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients: pembrolizumab -0.006 (p=0.39); SOTORASIB +0.323 (p<1e-300); olaparib +0.002 (p=0.73); osimertinib +0.001 (p=0.86). Only sotorasib has a non-null adjusted main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the treatment-by-biomarker interactions are added to the multivariable model (sotorasib×kras_g12c, olaparib×brca2_mutation, osimertinib×egfr_mutation, pembrolizumab×tmb_high, pembrolizumab×pdl1_tps), only sotorasib×kras_g12c remains significant.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficients: sotorasib:kras_g12c +2.535 (p<1e-300, SIGNIFICANT); olaparib:brca2_mutation -0.034 (p=0.34); osimertinib:egfr_mutation -0.022 (p=0.22); pembrolizumab:tmb_high -0.001 (p=0.95); pembrolizumab:pdl1_tps -0.002 (p=0.94). Confirms sotorasib×kras as the only canonical biomarker-drug effect present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner CATE analysis (random forests fit separately to treated vs untreated, predict difference) followed by a depth-3 decision tree on the predicted CATE will (a) identify KRAS+ × male sex as the primary modifier of sotorasib effect; and (b) reveal essentially no clinically meaningful heterogeneity for pembrolizumab, olaparib, or osimertinib.

analyses.code: Random-forest T-learner per treatment; depth-3 DecisionTreeRegressor on CATE
analyses.result_summary: SOTORASIB CATE tree: top splits kras_g12c, then sex_female. KRAS+/male leaves: CATE +4.25 (non-squamous), +5.02 (squamous). KRAS+/female leaves: -0.29 to +0.29. KRAS- leaves: -0.12 to +0.28. Mean CATE 0.31, std 1.24. PEMBROLIZUMAB: top splits all on age, max leaf magnitude |CATE|<0.28; mean -0.01, std 0.29. OLAPARIB: top splits histology/brain mets/age; leaf range -0.06 to +0.20; mean 0.008, std 0.31. OSIMERTINIB: top splits has_brain_mets/age; leaf range -0.22 to +0.45; mean 0.006, std 0.34. Hypothesis fully supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 subgroup (KRAS+ males, n=3,508), treatment_sotorasib increases mean pfs_months by approximately 4.6 months versus no sotorasib, irrespective of histology.

analyses.code: ttest in df[(kras_g12c==1)&(sex_female==0)] by treatment_sotorasib, then by histology
analyses.result_summary: Overall in KRAS+ males: on n=1237 mean 7.88, off n=2271 mean 3.23, diff=+4.64 mo, p<1e-300. By histology: adeno on 8.16 vs off 3.49, diff=+4.67 mo (p~3e-255); squamous on 7.35 vs off 2.71, diff=+4.64 mo (p~8e-147). Effect is consistent across histology. The KRAS+ male subgroup contains 7.0% of the cohort and accounts for essentially the entire population-level sotorasib effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS+ females (n=2,860), no patient subgroup shows a clinically meaningful (>0.3 mo) sotorasib treatment effect.

analyses.code: Stratified ttests in df[(kras_g12c==1)&(sex_female==1)] across single-feature subgroups
analyses.result_summary: All sotorasib on-vs-off differences in KRAS+ female subgroups remain near zero (|diff|<0.3 mo). The lack of benefit in KRAS+ females is robust and not rescued by any other feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The dataset's strongest prognostic factor for pfs_months is age_years, with each additional year of age adding approximately 0.16 months of PFS in the multivariable model — opposite the usual real-world direction.
Stage IV disease and ECOG performance status are strong negative prognostic factors for pfs_months independent of treatment.

analyses.code: Adjusted multivariable OLS, coefficient on age_years
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age_years coefficient ~+0.169 mo per year (p<1e-300). Direction is positive, opposite to clinical expectation that older age worsens outcomes — likely a synthetic-data quirk but a robust feature of THIS cohort.
stage_iv coefficient ~ -1.4 mo (p<1e-300); ecog_ps coefficient ~ -1.1 mo per unit (p<1e-300); has_brain_mets ~ -0.9 mo (p<1e-300); squamous histology ~ -0.86 mo. All as expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final synthesis: the only clinically meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity in this dataset is treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female, with positive effect concentrated in patients meeting all three predicates (kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0), magnitude ≈ +4.6 months PFS, additionally amplified by older age (+0.03 mo per year). No subgroup yields a positive treatment effect for pembrolizumab (with or without conditioning on tmb_high or pdl1_tps), olaparib (with or without brca2), or osimertinib (with or without egfr) in this dataset.

analyses.result_summary: All prior analyses converge on this conclusion. Sotorasib in KRAS+ males: diff=+4.64 mo, p<1e-300, n=3,508. All other treatment-biomarker pairings null at clinical magnitude. Best-supported subgroup hypothesis for treatment-effect heterogeneity: {kras_g12c=1, sex_female=0} → treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by ~4.6 mo (positive), modulated upward by age.
